text:
Dutch child rapist Steven van de Velde is being housed in private accommodation away from theOlympicvillage at the Paris 2024 Games. The beach volleyball player - who was convicted for raping a British girl in 2014 - is being “safeguarded” by the Dutch Olympic Committee (DOC).

Van de Velde was jailed in 2016 for raping a 12-year-old on British soil, receiving a four-year prison sentence but only serving 12 months. The 29-year-old was controversially picked for the Dutch Olympic volleyball team in June and arrived in France last week under tight security.

His Olympic debut on Sunday at Champs de Mars park under the Eiffel Tower was met with boos from the crowd as he and teammate Matthew Immers lost to Italy. And it has been reported byThe Athleticthat Van de Velde requested to stay off-site for the duration of the Games, away from the thousands of other athletes.



It is claimed that the DOC agreed to the arrangement to shield him from public scrutiny and media questioning. "They (the Dutch Olympic Committee) have put out a statement, they've made it very clear there's a lot of safeguarding going on, special extra safeguarding," International Olympic Committee spokesman Mark Adams told a news conference in Paris on Saturday.

The DOC outlined its position last Thursday in a statement, which said: "We are implementing concrete measures to ensure a safe sporting environment for all participants. These measures include, at the request of Van de Velde, alternative accommodation for Van de Velde and no media contact during his stay in Paris."

Following Italy's victory over the Dutch team, with scores of 22-20, 19-21, 15-13, Van de Velde left the premises on his own. His next match with Immers is scheduled for 31 July at 15:00 BST against Chilean duo Marco and Esteban Grimalt.

Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.



Van de Velde's crime dates back to an encounter on Facebook which led to him travelling from Amsterdam to the UK in 2014, where he raped a girl at an address in Milton Keynes. A European warrant was issued for Van de Velde's arrest and he was extradited to the UK in January 2016, with the court case held in March of the same year.

Van de Velde pleaded guilty to three counts of child rape - specifically the "rape of a child under the age of 13". The athlete received a four-year prison sentence in 2016.

He later returned to the Netherlands, where he underwent a "specialist treatment programme". Van de Velde has since married fellow professional volleyball player Kim Behrens, who also serves as a police officer in Germany, and with whom he shares a son.

title: Dutch rapist Steven van de Velde keeping clear of Olympic village | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Kasra Moradi
description: Convicted child rapist Steven van de Velde is staying in private accommodation away from the Olympic village at the Paris Games as a 'safeguarding' measure.
